rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JESUS PULE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/4/24. (No earlier availability.) The second amended accounting was just e-filed on 8/29/24 despite counsel having been told that it needed to be filed well before this hearing in order for notice of it to be given timely for this hearing. In light of the fairly recent substitution of attorney for one of the co-conservators, counsel of record for the other two co-conservators will not be required to appear; however, if a courtesy copy of the second amended accounting petition, proof of service of notice of it for the hearing on 11/4/24, bank statements, and a proposed order are not received by the examiner by 10/23/24, an appearance will be required on 11/4/24. A trustee or a beneficiary of the trust may petition the court to settle the accounts and pass upon the acts of the trustee, including the exercise of the trustee's discretionary powers. [Probate Code Section 17200(b)(5).] This is a conservatorship proceeding, and the trust has not been brought before this Court.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE SOPHIE VERGARA SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/6/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) The trustee was ordered on 11/28/22 to apply the $54,248.28 it overpaid itself in the first two accounting periods to any compensation allowed to the trustee in this third accounting period. That does not appear to have been done. (3) There are no "extraordinary" services in trust administration. Extraordinary services refer to services in decedents' estates beyond those covered by ordinary services. (4) For what legal services were fees totaling $435 paid to the law firm on 11/15/23? (5) Disbursements to a third party to prepare the Court-conformed accounting must be deducted from the trustee's compensation. (6) Where was the trustee authorized to hold investments itself and pay itself advisory fees on top of trustee fees? (7) Are the parents not required to pay for any of Sophie's needs or even little luxuries? The SNT pays for even her swimsuit, stickers, and birthday party decorations? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/28/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The order dated 12/20/18 did NOT establish the SNT, which was why a petition was required to be filed in the correct department to have a proposed SNT approved.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RAYMOND BESSARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/4/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of publication has been filed. (2) Both the mailed and published notice must clearly indicate that the petitioner seeks to have a LOST will admitted. (3) The Notice of Petition to Administer Estate was incomplete at item 6b. (4) The proof of service was verified on 8/9/24, before the notice was mailed on 8/17/24, making the verification fallacious. (5) Notice of Hearing must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (6) If the petitioner seeks to be appointed as the executor, the petition caption is incorrect. (7) If the estate value is estimated to be only $10,000, why is a formal estate needed? (8) If the will is lost, item 3f(3) of the petition should have been checked. (Item 3f(2) of the petition is also incomplete, if the lost will was self-proving... but the will does not appear to have been completed by the testator.) (9) Items 5a(3) or (4), 5a(5)(a) or (b), 5a(7) or (8), and 5b of the petition must be completed. (10) The Receipt and Acknowledgement of Duties and Liabilities is incomplete. (11) Evidence is required to overcome the presumption of revocation. Probate Code Sect. 6124. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 10/24/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HIDEO IWATA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/6/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice of this hearing of the third amended petition was mailed on 8/26/24, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. (2) The "supplement" filed on 8/27/24 AMENDED the proposed distribution. An amending pleading, regardless of the caption, requires notice. [CRC 7.53(a).] Mailing notice on 8/28/24 for a hearing on 9/9/24 is insufficient. (3) The 13100 affidavits do not appear to have a certified copy of the death certificate or the mandatory JC form DE-300 attached to them. [Probate Code Section 11802(c) and LRSF 14.35 G.] (4) Since the administrator has failed to file a proper I&A and is relying on the conservator's accounting, and the receipt filed by the conservator showed $573,186.41 in cash received by this personal representative, that will be the cash included in the fee base, as that is apparently the only cash the administrator actually managed. (5) Please file proof of the sale proceeds for the items of tangible personal property and currency and coins. (6) No receipt has been filed for the small books reportedly received by Shigeo Iwata. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/25/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The administrator was granted IAEA authority on 12/6/21. The copy of the third amended petition provided to the examiner was not a true and correct copy; it was not signed or verified by anyone.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDWARD PINEIRO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the sole purpose of determining if Letters of Special Administration will need to be extended. 1) No will (or copy of the lost will) has been lodged. 2) The proof of service filed on 8/7/24 (i) was incomplete at items 2 and 3, (ii) did not clearly indicate that a LOST will was to be admitted, and (iii) did not have a printout of the instructions for how to appear remotely attached to the notice. 3) The lost 1981 will is not self-proving. 4) The other persons mentioned in the will (Mary L. Pineiro and Greta Helen La Croix) are missing from item 8 of the petition. 5) The date of death of any deceased heir or beneficiary must be set forth. [LRSF 14.18 C. and D.] 6) Petitioner alleges that she is Greta Helen La Croix's "only surviving child," which does not explicitly address whether Greta Helen La Croix had any other issue (notwithstanding the allegation in item 6b of the petition). Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDWARD PINEIRO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Petitioner alleged that there were no known next of kin. Now that a niece has filed her own petition (and contest of this 2023 will), he will need to serve notice to her if he intends to proceed with his petition. 2) One of the witnesses to the will shares the same last name as Petitioner. Is she related to Petitioner? Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.
